note: Do not instantiate directly; use itasca.structure module functions.

method_groups.normal: normal_dir, normal_disp, normal_stiffness, normal_strength, normal_stress, set_normal_strength
method_groups.shear: shear_cohesion, shear_dir, shear_disp, shear_friction, shear_residual, shear_state, shear_stiffness, shear_stress

methods.name: area
methods.description: Get surface area of the Liner element.

methods.name: beta
methods.description: Get Liner system beta angle. This is defined as the angle between the element local system x-axis and the material system x-axis. In radians measured counter-clockwise from the element local x-axis.

methods.name: component_id
methods.description: Get the Liner component id.

methods.name: connectivity
methods.description: Get the index of the node with component-ID id in the element. A ValueError exception is raised if no node in that element matches.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete this structural element.

methods.name: density
methods.description: Get the density of this structural element.

methods.name: depth_factor
methods.description: Get the depth factor at which stresses have been recovered. The depth equals Ft/2 where t is the Liner element thickness. F values equal to +1.0 or -1.0 correspond to the outer/inner Liner surface. The outer surface is defined by the positive z-direction of the local Liner element system. F values of 0.0 corresponds to the Liner mid-surface. The depth factor can be modified with the \(structure Liner recover stress\) command.

methods.name: element_type
methods.description: Get the name of the finite-element type used by this Liner element. The possible types for Liner elements are ‘cst’, ‘csth’, ‘dkt’, ‘dkt-cst’, and ‘dkt-csth’.

methods.name: embedded
methods.description: Get the embedded flag. True denotes that the liner element is embedded.

methods.name: extra
methods.description: Get the Liner extra data in the given slot.

methods.name: force_nodal
methods.description: Get the generalized nodal force acting on the Liner at each node. The translational and rotational components of node 1 are given first.

methods.name: group
methods.description: Get the Liner group name in a given slot.

methods.name: group_remove
methods.signature: liner.group_remove(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> bool
methods.description: Remove from the given group from all group slots of the Liner. One argument of type string, giving the group name, is required. The return value is a bool which is True if the group was removed from any slot, otherwise False.

methods.name: groups
methods.description: Get a dictionary describing which groups this Liner is part of. The keys of the dictionary are the slot names and the values are the group names.

methods.name: id
methods.description: Get the Liner structural element id.

methods.name: in_group
methods.signature: liner.in_group(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> bool
methods.description: Test if the Liner is part of a given group. If the optional argument slot is given, only that slot is searched. Otherwise, all group slots are searched.

methods.name: local_system
methods.description: Get local coordinate system of this element as a 3x3 tensor.

methods.name: mark
methods.description: Get the mark flag on the structural element. The structural element logic may modify this flag during command processing and cycling.

methods.name: nodes
methods.signature: liner.nodes() -> tuple of Structural element node objects
methods.description: Get a tuple containing the structural element nodes attached to this elemment.

returns.type: tuple of Structural element node objects

methods.name: normal_dir
methods.description: Get the direction (unit vector in global system) in which the liner element is being loaded by the normal coupling spring at each node.

methods.name: normal_disp
methods.description: Get the displacement magnitude in the normal coupling spring at each node. The coupling spring lies in the tangent plane of the liner surface. The direction in which force is acting is given by \(normal_dir\) method.

methods.name: normal_stiffness
methods.description: Get the normal coupling spring stiffness of the liner element for each side of the liner.

methods.name: normal_strength
methods.description: Get the normal coupling spring tensile strength for each side of the liner element.

methods.name: normal_stress
methods.description: Get the stress magnitude in the normal coupling spring at each node. The coupling spring lies in the tangent plane of the liner surface. The direction in which force is acting is given by \(normal_dir\) method.

methods.name: poisson
methods.description: Get the Poisson’s ratio of the element.

methods.name: pos
methods.description: Get the position vector of the element, defined as the element centroid.

methods.name: pressure
methods.description: Get the uniform pressure acting on the Liner. A positive value acts in the z-direction of the element local system.

methods.name: prop_anis
methods.signature: liner.prop_anis() -> (membrane: tuple of float, bending: tuple of float)
methods.description: Get the anisotropic material properties. Material-stiffness coefficients c’11, c’12, c’13, c’22, c’23, c’33 are returned for both the membrane and bending stiffnesses in that order. If the element does not have anisotropic properties, zeros are returned.

returns.type: (membrane: tuple of float, bending: tuple of float)

methods.name: prop_ortho
methods.signature: liner.prop_ortho() -> (membrane: tuple of float, bending: tuple of float)
methods.description: Get the orthotropic material properties. Material-stiffness coefficients c’11, c’12, c’13, c’22, c’23, c’33 are returned for both the membrane and bending stiffnesses in that order. If the element does not have orthotropic properties, zeros are returned.

returns.type: (membrane: tuple of float, bending: tuple of float)

methods.name: prop_type
methods.description: Get the material property type. Possible return values are ‘isotropic’, ‘orthotripc’, and ‘anisotropic’.

methods.name: resultant
methods.signature: liner.resultant(location: int) -> length 8 tuple of float
methods.description: Get stress resultants last calculated for the element at the index \(location\). A \(location\) value of 0 gives the resultant at the element centroid; \(location\) values 1, 2 and 3 give the resultant at the nodes. The components in the return value are: Mx, My, Mxy, Nx, Ny, Nxy, Qx and Qy. Note that stress resultants must be calculated with the \(structure Liner recover\) command. See also the \(resultant_valid\) method.

methods.name: resultant_valid
methods.description: Get stress resultant validity flag. True indicates that the stress resultant values are valid. False indicates that resultants have not been calculated or that there has been a change since they were last calculated that would invalidate the results.

methods.name: set_density
methods.description: Set the density of this structural element.

methods.name: set_extra
methods.description: Set the Liner extra data in the given slot.

methods.name: set_group
methods.signature: liner.set_group(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> None
methods.description: Set the Liner group name in a given slot.

methods.name: set_mark
methods.description: Set the mark flag on the structural element. The structural element logic may modify this flag during command processing and cycling.

methods.name: set_normal_strength
methods.description: Set the normal coupling spring tensile strength for each side of the liner element.

methods.name: set_pressure
methods.description: Set the uniform pressure acting on the Liner. A positive value acts in the z-direction of the element local system.

methods.name: set_slide
methods.description: Set the large-strain sliding flag. A true value denotes that the large-strain sliding is on.

methods.name: set_slide_tol
methods.description: Set the (shear) coupling spring slide tolerance of the liner element.

methods.name: set_thermal_expansion
methods.description: Set the thermal-expansion coefficient for the structural element.

methods.name: set_thickness
methods.description: Set the element thickness.

methods.name: shear_cohesion
methods.description: Get the shear coupling spring cohesion for each side of the liner element.

methods.name: shear_dir
methods.description: Get the direction (unit vector in global system) in which the liner element is being loaded by the shear coupling spring at each node.

methods.name: shear_disp
methods.description: Get the displacement magnitude in the shear coupling spring at each node. The coupling spring lies in the tangent plane of the liner surface. The direction in which force is acting is given by \(shear_dir\) method.

methods.name: shear_friction
methods.description: Get the shear coupling spring friction angle for each side of the liner element.

methods.name: shear_residual
methods.description: Get the shear coupling spring residual cohesion for each side of the liner element.

methods.name: shear_state
methods.signature: liner.shear_state() -> (int, int, int, int, int, int)
methods.description: Get the yield state of the coupling spring at each side of each node. Return values 1, 2 and 3 denotes never yielded, yielding now, or yielded in the past, respectively. Side 1 and 2 of node 1 are given first.

methods.name: shear_stiffness
methods.description: Get the shear coupling spring stiffness for each side of the liner element.

methods.name: shear_stress
methods.description: Get the stress magnitude in the coupling spring at each node. The coupling spring lies in the tangent plane of the liner surface. The direction in which force is acting is given by the \(shear_dir\) method.

methods.name: slide
methods.description: Get the large-strain sliding flag. A true value denotes that the large-strain sliding is on.

methods.name: slide_tol
methods.description: Get the (shear) coupling spring slide tolerance of the liner element.

methods.name: stress
methods.description: Get the Liner stresses at the current depth factor at the index \(location\). A \(location\) value of 0 gives the stress at the element centroid; \(location\) values 1, 2 and 3 give the stress at the nodes. Stresses are expressed in the global system. Note that compressive stresses are negative.

methods.name: stress_max
methods.description: Get the maximum stress of the element. This is the maximum principal stress of the fiber stress at depth +1 or -1, which is the combination of membrane and bending resultants.

methods.name: stress_prin
methods.description: Get the resolved principal stress at the current depth factor at the index \(location\). A \(location\) value of 0 gives the stress at the element centroid; \(location\) values 1, 2 and 3 give the stress at the nodes. Returns a vector encoded as (minimum, intermediate, maximum). Note that compressive stresses are negative in FLAC3D.

methods.name: stress_valid
methods.description: Get the stress validity flag for the element. True indicates that the stresses last calculated at the current depth factor are valid. Galse indicates that stresses have not been calculated, or that there has been a change that invalidates the last stresses calculated.

methods.name: thermal_expansion
methods.description: Get the thermal-expansion coefficient for the structural element.

methods.name: thickness
methods.description: Get the element thickness.

methods.name: valid
methods.description: Returns True if this Liner is live.

methods.name: volume
methods.description: Get the element volume, or surface area times thickness.

methods.name: young
methods.description: Get the Youngs modulus of the element. Modifying the value during cycling may be a danger.
